title: Mastering the MERN Stack in 2026
excerpt: A deep dive into architecting scalable, real-time applications using MongoDB, Express, React 19, and Node.js.
content:
# Getting Started with the MERN Stack

The MERN stack is one of the most popular full-stack JavaScript frameworks. It consists of:

- **MongoDB** — NoSQL database for flexible data storage
- **Express.js** — Backend web framework for Node.js
- **React.js** — Frontend library for building user interfaces
- **Node.js** — JavaScript runtime for server-side development

## Why MERN?

The biggest advantage of MERN is that you use JavaScript across the entire stack. This means you can share code, logic, and even types between your frontend and backend.

## Setting Up Your First Project

Start by initializing a new Node.js project and installing the dependencies:

```bash
mkdir my-mern-app
cd my-mern-app
npm init -y
npm install express mongoose cors dotenv
```

Then create your Express server, connect to MongoDB, and build your React frontend. The MERN stack gives you everything you need to build modern web applications.

Stay tuned for more in-depth tutorials!

title: My Journey into Web Development
excerpt: From computer technician to full-stack developer — here's how I made the transition and what I learned along the way.
content:
# My Journey into Web Development

My path into web development wasn't a straight line. I started as a **Computer Technician** back in 2020, working with hardware and systems at Tech Ace Gaming & Computer Company.

## The Spark

While fixing computers and managing networks, I found myself increasingly drawn to the software side. I started learning HTML, CSS, and JavaScript in my spare time.

## Building Skills

I progressed through several roles — from network administration to IT support — all while quietly building my coding skills. Each role taught me something valuable:

- **Troubleshooting mindset** from tech support
- **System thinking** from network administration
- **User empathy** from working with non-technical users

## The Leap

When I started my internship at NK Software House as a React Developer, everything clicked. Building real applications with React.js was exactly what I wanted to do.

## Where I Am Now

Today, I'm a Full-Stack Developer specializing in the MERN stack, and a Project Coordinator at RITZ. The journey was long, but every step was worth it.

If you're thinking about making a similar transition, my advice is simple: **start building things**. The best way to learn is by doing.
